badge_template.description: This badge earner demonstrates knowledge about building user interfaces with IBM's open-source Carbon Design System React components. This badge earner demonstrates the ability to install Carbon React components in their project, use and populate these components with data, build custom components, and deploy a project to IBM Cloud.
badge_template.name: IBM Carbon Design System Developer Essentials - React

badge_template_activities.title: Completion of all steps of the Carbon React tutorial, including submission and approval of a Pull Request for each step.
For questions related to this badge or course, contact Matt Rosno (matt.rosno@ibm.com).
This badge is retired and no longer available.

badge_template.description: This badge earner can solve advanced mainframe problems by thinking critically and applying information from a wide variety of sources. They have orchestrated system activities using Ansible and Zowe, while working in Docker containerized environments. The earner has also demonstrated key mainframe skills, including REXX , COBOL, VSAM, TSO, and DFSMS commands.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2020 - Level 3

badge_template_activities.title: Complete Level 2 of Master the Mainframe 2020.
Complete Level 3 of Master the Mainframe 2020.

badge_template.description: The Computing Fundamentals examination covers a foundational understanding of computer hardware, software, operating systems, peripherals, and troubleshooting. It is designed to help you maximize the value and benefits of using computer technology.
badge_template.name: Computing Fundamentals

badge_template_activities.title: Identify types of computers, how they process information, and the purpose and function of different hardware components
Identify how to maintain computer equipment and solve common problems relating to computer hardware
Identify how software and hardware work together to perform computing tasks and how software is distributed and upgraded
Identify different types of application software and general concepts relating to application software categories
Identify what an operating system is and how it works, and solve common problems related to operating systems
Use an operating system to manipulate a computer’s desktop, files and disks, change system settings, and install and remove software.

badge_template.description: This badge earner has learned and displayed fundamental mainframe skills. This individual is able to perform everyday tasks in an IBM Z mainframe environment, including administering data sets, formulating commands, managing JCL, scripting actions in USS (Unix System Services), writing and debugging Python, and orchestrating actions through IBM Z Open Automation Utilities.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2020 - Level 2

badge_template_activities.title: Must successfully complete all challenges in Level 2 of the 2020 Master the Mainframe Contest or Learning System.

badge_template.description: The badge earner has the ability to innovate on the mainframe by solving real-life situations encountered by experienced systems programmers. This individual has demonstrated their extensive programming skills (advanced commands system navigation) and is able to develop application tasks (C, JAVA, COBOL, assembler and REXX) on the z/OS platform.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2019 - Part 3

badge_template_activities.title: Must currently be a student at a high school, college or university, and at least 16 years old.
Registration in the IBM Master the Mainframe contest for 2019.
Complete Master the Mainframe Part 2.
Complete Master the Mainframe - Part 3.

badge_template.description: The badge earner has mastered basic mainframe concepts. This individual is able to perform programming (advanced commands, system setup, and advanced system navigation) and application developing tasks (C, JAVA, COBOL, assembler and REXX) all on the z/OS platform.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2019 - Part 2

badge_template_activities.title: Must currently be a student at a high school, college or university, and at least 16 years old.
Registration in the IBM Master the Mainframe contest for 2019.
Completion of the IBM Master the Mainframe - Part 2 contest.

badge_template.description: The badge earner has demonstrated above average familiarity and practical experience with the z/OS environment, including: z/OS access and navigation of data sources; programming languages and JES spool using TSO, ISPF, JCL, SDSF, SSH, FTP, Unix Services, VSAM, DB2, Communications Server, RACF, and SMP/E; and identification of base components.
badge_template.name: z/OS Introduction & Workshop

badge_template_activities.title: Attending a z/OS Introduction Workshop.
Completion of all class labs.
Receiving a passing grade on the final exam.

badge_template.description: This badge earner has gained knowledge about the structure of COBOL programs, different data types and variable handling, intrinsic functions, branching logic and more. The individual is able to write basic COBOL programs.
badge_template.name: COBOL Programming with VSCode

badge_template_activities.title: Successful completion of the course Learning COBOL Programming with VSCode which includes hands-on training on IBM Mainframe z/OS environment.
Achieve a score of 80% or more on the final quiz at the end of the course.

badge_template.description: The badge earner has the ability to innovate on the mainframe by solving real life situations encountered by experienced systems programmers. This individual has demonstrated their extensive programming skills (advanced commands system navigation) and is able to develop application tasks (C, JAVA, COBOL, assembler and REXX), all on the z/OS platform.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2018 - Part 3

badge_template_activities.title: Must currently be a student at a high school, college or university, and at least 16 years old.
Registration in the IBM Master the Mainframe contest for 2018.
Completion of the IBM Master the Mainframe - Part 2 challenges.
Completion of the IBM Master the Mainframe - Part 3 challenges.
IBM Academic Initiative will contact you of your eligibility automatically with further instructions to receive this badge.
Note: This badge is retired and no longer available.

evidence.name: 2018 Master the Mainframe Wall of Fame

badge_template.description: The badge earner has mastered basic mainframe concepts. This individual is able to perform programming (advanced commands, system setup, and advanced system navigation) and application developing tasks (C, JAVA, COBOL, assembler and REXX) all on the z/OS platform.
badge_template.name: Master the Mainframe 2018 - Part 2

badge_template_activities.title: Must currently be a student at a high school, college or university, and at least 16 years old.
Registration in the IBM Master the Mainframe contest for 2018.
Completion of the IBM Master the Mainframe - Part 2 challenges.
IBM Academic Initiative will contact you of your eligibility automatically with further instructions to receive this badge.
Note: This badge is now retired and no longer available.
